# @juandinella/audio-bands

Headless audio analysis library for the browser. Returns normalized `bass`, `mid`, `high`, custom named bands, raw FFT bins, and time-domain waveform data without shipping a renderer.

## Install

```bash
npm install @juandinella/audio-bands
```

## What it solves

Most audio visualization libraries either handle only playback with no analysis, or draw their own canvas and hide the data. This library only gives you numbers — what you render is up to you.

## Vanilla JS

```js
import { AudioBands } from '@juandinella/audio-bands';

const audio = new AudioBands({
  customBands: {
    presence: { from: 0.25, to: 0.5 },
  },
  onLoadError: (error) => console.error(error),
});
await audio.load('/track.mp3');
await audio.play();

function loop() {
  const { bass, mid, high, overall } = audio.getBands();
  const custom = audio.getCustomBands();
  const fft = audio.getFftData(); // Uint8Array(128) — raw frequency bins
  requestAnimationFrame(loop);
}
requestAnimationFrame(loop);

audio.destroy(); // clean up when done
```

## React hook

```tsx
import { useAudioBands } from '@juandinella/audio-bands/react';

const { loadTrack, play, pause, getBands, getCustomBands, getFftData, getWaveform, isPlaying, micActive, loadError, micError } = useAudioBands();

// Call getBands / getCustomBands / getFftData / getWaveform inside requestAnimationFrame
```

## Key API

- `getBands(source?)` — returns `{ bass, mid, high, overall }` for `'music'` (default) or `'mic'`
- `getCustomBands(source?)` — returns normalized values for configured custom bands
- `getFftData(source?)` — returns raw `Uint8Array` of 128 frequency bins (0–255 each)
- `getWaveform(source?)` — returns time-domain waveform for `'music'` (default) or `'mic'` as `Uint8Array`
- `enableMic()` / `disableMic()` — mic input, not connected to destination (no feedback)
- `load(url)` / `play()` / `pause()` — transport controls split from analyser setup
- `getState()` — returns playback, mic, and error state
- `destroy()` — closes AudioContext and stops mic stream

## Notes

- `AudioContext` is created lazily on first `load()` or `enableMic()` call — respects browser autoplay policy
- `load()` prepares the current track; call `play()` or `togglePlayPause()` to start playback
- Root export is framework-agnostic, `@juandinella/audio-bands/core` is explicit core-only, and `@juandinella/audio-bands/react` exposes the hook
- React is an optional peer dependency

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@juandinella/audio-bands
- GitHub: https://github.com/juandinella/audio-bands
- Demo: https://audio-bands.juandinella.com
